meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn how to implement analytics setup to validate SaaS ideas quickly using Antigravity and Claude Code for beginners.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing abstract ideas. Use analytics to track user friction in existing apps, revealing high-value pain points. By instrumenting a simple event-tracking layer, you transform guesswork into data-driven validation. This approach ensures you build features users are already signaling they need, drastically increasing your SaaS monetization potential.
content.intro:
The blank screen of the IDE is a founder’s greatest enemy, but the real tragedy isn't a lack of ideas—it’s a lack of signal. Most builders treat their initial projects like lottery tickets, hoping for market fit. This is the path to burnout. The elite approach is to stop 'inventing' and start 'observing.'

By embedding a lean analytics layer into your initial prototype, you turn your MVP into a diagnostic tool. You aren't just building software; you are running a business experiment. We’ll show you how to leverage AI tools to automate this instrumentation, ensuring every line of code you write is backed by actionable user behavior data.

steps.title: Define User Intent Events
steps.description: Identify the core friction points where a user might drop off. We will define a schema for tracking 'conversion' and 'exit' events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a lightweight analytics tracker that captures user clicks, page duration, and feature usage without slowing down the initial load time.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create event_tracker.ts utility with PostHog or segment-style interface'

steps.title: Automate Event Instrumentation
steps.description: Use AI to inject the tracker across your existing components, ensuring 100% coverage without manual slog.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan my current React components and wrap all primary call-to-action buttons with the trackEvent function defined in the previous step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code --apply 'Instrument all buttons in /src/components with tracking events'

steps.title: Setup Data Visualization Dashboard
steps.description: Connect your tracking to a simple dashboard to visualize where users are actually spending their time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a simple dashboard view using Recharts that aggregates the event data stored in my local storage/backend to show most popular features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate AnalyticsDashboard.tsx component to aggregate and visualize user events'

steps.title: Implement Feedback Loops
steps.description: Add a micro-survey trigger when a user hits a specific high-friction event.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a conditional modal that triggers a 'What was missing here?' survey when a user clicks a disabled or empty-state feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code --add-feature 'FeatureFeedbackModal triggered on empty state click'

steps.title: Validate and Pivot
steps.description: Analyze the data to decide whether to double down or pivot the feature set.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current analytics log file and identify the top 3 features users interact with, then suggest a roadmap for the next sprint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude analyze --file ./data/analytics_logs.json --query 'What are the top 3 high-friction areas?'

pro_tips: Never track everything; track the 'Aha!' moment and the 'Drop-off' point to save on storage and processing.
Use session replay tools alongside event tracking for context on how users navigate your UI.
Treat your analytics schema as a product requirement document (PRD) that evolves with your features.
Automate your analytics reporting to email you weekly; data you don't look at is data you wasted money collecting.
Prioritize user privacy from day one; anonymize all user IDs to maintain compliance and build trust.
content.next_steps: Review your first week of data, identify the highest bounce rate, and use Claude Code to re-engineer that specific user journey for higher conversion.
content.conversion_block: Don't build in the dark. Use data to validate your vision and secure your first paying customers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
